opt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Opata.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
pit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pitta-Pitta.
pity: noun. A feeling of sympathy at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.
port: noun. A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.
pot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Potawatomi.
pry: verb. To peer closely and curiously, especially at something closed or not public.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
ropy: adjective. Resembling rope in appearance or texture, used especially of muscles that are thick or hard to the touch.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.
tip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Trimuris.
top: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Papantla Totonac.
topi: noun. An antelope of the species Damaliscus korrigum syn.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
torii: noun. A traditional Japanese gate at Shinto shrines, symbolically marking the transition from the profane to the sacred.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
trip: noun. A journey; an excursion or jaunt.
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
try: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Turung.
yip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pholo.
